preamble: The transformation of the diphthong 'ou' into the voiced labio-velar approximant 'w' is a process of consonantization or glide formation. This typically occurs when the vocalic elements of the diphthong, particularly the high back rounded off-glide, become more consonantal in nature, often in specific phonetic environments such as before another vowel.

examples.note: In Nubi, the spelling 'ou' can correspond phonetically to either /w/ or /u/. This indicates a historical development where a sound represented by 'ou' has shifted to a labio-velar approximant in certain contexts.

sources: Tosco, M., & Owens, J. (1993). Linguistic studies in Nubi. Rüdiger Köppe Verlag.
